intro.title: The Last Pagan Emperor's Brother-in-law
intro.body: John I Tzimiskes campaigned south into Hamdanid Syria and Fatimid territory in 974-975, recovering relics from Antioch and reaching as far as the Sea of Galilee. The expedition is recorded in his letter to King Ashot III of Armenia preserved by Matthew of Edessa.

stops.name: Constantinople
stops.note: Spring 974. The emperor's letter dates the army's muster to Lazarus Saturday.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Tagmatic regiments parading in the Hippodrome, the Mese road decorated for Easter.

stops.name: Antioch
stops.note: Already in Byzantine hands since 969 (Phokas reconquest). Tzimiskes uses it as forward base; he recovers the sandals of Christ kept here.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Antioch citadel with imperial standards, relic procession through the colonnaded street.

stops.name: Baalbek
stops.note: Taken October 974. Tzimiskes's letter says he found Roman ruins still standing and many Muslim sanctuaries.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Hexagonal temple ruins beside a fortified Muslim town, Byzantine cavalry encamped between.

stops.name: Damascus
stops.note: Fatimid governor pays tribute and submits, autumn 974. The city itself was not occupied.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Damascus gate with white-robed governor handing over keys, Byzantine officers receiving.

stops.name: Tiberias
stops.note: Sea of Galilee — southernmost reach of the campaign, spring 975. Tzimiskes pauses for pilgrimage at the holy sites.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured plate. Lake Tiberias at first light, emperor in pilgrim's cloak at the shore, basilica behind.
